mcqs.question: In a raster scan display system, the electron beam scans the screen:

options.text: In a random pattern to draw vectors
Row by row from top to bottom
From center to edges in concentric circles
Diagonally from corner to corner

mcqs.explanation: Raster scan systematically sweeps the electron beam horizontally across each row (scan line), moving from top to bottom of the screen to paint pixels.

mcqs.question: What distinguishes random scan (vector) display systems from raster scan systems?

options.text: Random scan uses liquid crystal technology
Random scan draws lines directly by directing the beam to specific endpoints
Random scan cannot display color images
Random scan has a fixed resolution of 640x480 pixels

mcqs.explanation: Random scan systems direct the electron beam to draw vectors between specified endpoints, unlike raster scan which fills the entire screen row by row.

mcqs.question: A raster scan display has a resolution of 1024×768 pixels and a refresh rate of 60 Hz. What is the minimum required bandwidth for the video signal if each pixel requires 3 bytes of color information?

options.text: Approximately 141 MB/s
Approximately 1.7 GB/s
Approximately 141 Mbps
Approximately 1.7 GB/s

mcqs.explanation: Bandwidth = 1024 × 768 × 60 × 3 bytes ≈ 141 MB/s (or ~1.1 Gbps), representing the data transfer rate needed to refresh the display.

mcqs.question: Which of the following is an advantage of raster scan displays over random scan (vector) displays?

options.text: Smoother curves and filled polygons
Lower hardware complexity for color display
True representation of solid areas and textures
Higher brightness for outdoor use

mcqs.explanation: Raster scan can represent solid areas and textures by illuminating individual pixels, whereas random scan only draws wireframe lines.

mcqs.question: In a CRT-based raster display, the 'shadow mask' technique is primarily used to:

options.text: Increase the refresh rate to 120 Hz
Ensure accurate color registration by blocking misdirected electron beams
Reduce power consumption by 50%
Enable 3D stereoscopic viewing

mcqs.explanation: The shadow mask is a perforated metal plate that ensures each electron beam (red, green, blue) strikes only its corresponding phosphor dot, improving color purity.

mcqs.question: A designer needs to display a complex engineering drawing with fine detail and smooth lines at 90 Hz refresh rate. Which display technology would be MOST suitable and why?

options.text: Traditional CRT raster scan - because it naturally supports high refresh rates
Random scan CRT - because it excels at smooth line rendering of vectors
LCD panel - because it has no refresh rate limitation
Any CRT will work equally well for this application

mcqs.explanation: Random scan CRT systems are ideal for wireframe CAD applications as they draw precise lines directly without pixelation, maintaining smoothness at high refresh rates.

mcqs.question: In a color raster scan system with a frame buffer of 4 MB memory, assuming a color depth of 24 bits per pixel, what is the maximum display resolution that can be supported without compression?

options.text: 800 × 600 pixels
1024 × 768 pixels
1280 × 1024 pixels
1920 × 1080 pixels

mcqs.explanation: 4 MB = 4 × 2^20 bytes = 4,194,304 bytes. At 24 bits (3 bytes) per pixel: 4,194,304 / 3 ≈ 1,398,101 pixels. √1,398,101 ≈ 1182, so 1024×768 = 786,432 pixels fits within memory.

mcqs.question: An automotive dashboard display system must render real-time gauge readings, navigation maps, and warning icons simultaneously. The system experiences visible flicker at 50 Hz refresh. A designer proposes increasing to 60 Hz while maintaining 1920×1080 resolution at 24-bit color. Calculate the required frame buffer memory bandwidth (in GB/s) and recommend the best display technology.

options.text: 3.34 GB/s - Use LCD TFT as it eliminates flicker entirely
3.34 GB/s - Use OLED for true black and infinite contrast with no smear
0.6 GB/s - Use plasma display for fast response times
3.34 GB/s - Use high-quality LCD with overdrive technology to eliminate motion blur

mcqs.explanation: Bandwidth = 1920 × 1080 × 60 × 3 bytes = 373,248,000 bytes ≈ 0.35 GB/s per frame; with overhead ~3.34 GB/s. Modern LCD with overdrive provides fast response, high resolution, and eliminates flicker at 60 Hz while meeting automotive durability requirements.
